Items.Title: 混音和创建新帖子 | Remix and creating new posts
Items.Description:
很酷，所以我们刚刚将 Postgres 添加到了我们的 Remix 应用程序中。 让我们看看如何通过 Web 界面将新帖子添加到我们的数据库中。

本文的结果将是一个出色的 Web 表单，我们可以通过它向 Postgres 数据库添加新帖子。

创建表单

首先，创建一个名为routes/posts/new.tsx的超级简单的路由。

现在在里面创建表格。

 import { Form } from ' @remix-run/react ' ; const inputClassName = `w-full rounded border border-gray-500 px-2 py-1 text-lg` ; export default function NewPost () { return ( < Form method = ' post ' > < p > < label > Post Title :{ ' ' } < input type = ' text ' name = ' title ' className = { inputClassName } / > < /label > < /p > < p > < label > Post Slug :{ ' ' } < input type = ' text ' name = ' slug ' className = { inputClassName } / > < /label > < /p > < p className = ' text-right ' > < button type = ' submit ' className = ' rounded bg-blue-500 py-2 px-4 text-white hover:bg-blue-600 focus:bg-blue-400 disabled:bg-blue-300 ' > Create Post < /button > < /p > < p > < label > Content :{ ' ' } < input type = ' text ' name = ' content ' className = { inputClassName } / > < /label > < /p > < /Form > ); }

注意<Form>标签是一个混音模块吗？

让我们运行应用程序来看看它的外观。

很好，表格在那里！

处理数据

使用 Remix 表单最酷的部分是它会自动附带一个我们可以挂钩的动作。

它看起来像这样：

 export const action = async ({ request }) => { // Do a action };

在我们的例子中，这个动作是创建帖子，我们可以利用我们已经创建的post.server.ts文件。

 export const action = async ({ request }) => { const formData = await request . formData (); const title = formData . get ( ' title ' ); const slug = formData . get ( ' slug ' ); const content = formData . get ( ' content ' ); await createPost ({ title , slug , content }); return redirect ( ' /posts ' ); };

在这里，我们从表单中获取所有特定字段，并通过设置所有道具来调用createPost方法。

函数本身可能如下所示：

 export async function createPost ( post ) { return prisma . post . create ({ data : post }); }

是的，这就是你所需要的！

重新运行您的应用程序，填写表格并见证奇迹发生。

注意：它不会捕获任何错误； 我会把它留给你补充。

你可以在GitHub 上找到完整的代码。

感谢您的阅读，让我们联系吧！

感谢您阅读我的博客。 随时订阅我的电子邮件通讯并在Facebook或Twitter 上联系



原文:

Cool, so we just added Postgres to our Remix app. Let's see how we can add new posts to our database via the web interface.

The result of this article will be an excellent web form through which we can add a new post to our Postgres database.

Creating the form

First, create a super simple route called routes/posts/new.tsx.

Inside create the form for now.

import { Form } from '@remix-run/react'; const inputClassName = `w-full rounded border border-gray-500 px-2 py-1 text-lg`; export default function NewPost() { return ( <Form method='post'> <p> <label> Post Title:{' '} <input type='text' name='title' className={inputClassName} />
 </label>
 </p>
 <p> <label> Post Slug:{' '} <input type='text' name='slug' className={inputClassName} />
 </label>
 </p>
 <p className='text-right'> <button type='submit' className='rounded bg-blue-500 py-2 px-4 text-white hover:bg-blue-600 focus:bg-blue-400 disabled:bg-blue-300' > Create Post </button>
 </p>
 <p> <label> Content:{' '} <input type='text' name='content' className={inputClassName} />
 </label>
 </p>
 </Form>
 );
}

Note how the <Form> tag is a remix module?

Let's run the app to see how it looks.

Nice, the form is there!

Handling the data

And the cool part about using the Remix form is that it automatically comes with an action we can hook.

It would look like this:

export const action = async ({ request }) => { // Do a action
};

In our case, this action is to create the post, for which we can leverage the post.server.ts file we already created.

export const action = async ({ request }) => { const formData = await request.formData(); const title = formData.get('title'); const slug = formData.get('slug'); const content = formData.get('content'); await createPost({ title, slug, content }); return redirect('/posts');
};

Here we get all the specific fields from the form and invoke the createPost method by setting all the props.

The function itself can look like this:

export async function createPost(post) { return prisma.post.create({ data: post });
}

And yes, that will be all you need!

Rerun your app, fill out the form and see the magic happen.

Note: It won't catch any error; I'll leave that to you to add.

You can find the completed code on GitHub.

Thank you for reading, and let's connect!

Thank you for reading my blog. Feel free to subscribe to my email newsletter and connect on Facebook or Twitter

Items.Title: 2022 年面向开发人员的 13 大 Vue Devtools | Top 13 Vue Devtools for Developers in 2022
Items.Description:
它是一个用于设计任何用户界面的渐进式 JavaScript 框架，在 GitHub 上有 195k 星。

与市场上的其他框架相比，Vue 具有极高的可定制性。 这意味着您不必完全理解所有原则。 它在很短的时间内受到了程序员的广泛关注。

它是从头开始构建的，可以根据您的需要同时用作库和框架。 它由一个以视图层为中心的核心库和一个补充库的生态系统组成。 尽管如此，VueJS 已经成为最流行的 js 框架，因为它具有众多的特性和优点。

与 Angular、Backbone 和 React 等更复杂的前端框架相比，它相对紧凑、轻量且易于使用。 此外，Vue.js 在开发人员中迅速普及。

此外， Vue因其在线应用程序而受到大多数知名品牌的信任。 因此，将它用于您的业务可能是一个很好的策略。 一家定制的 Web 应用程序开发公司配备了必要的技能集，可以为您的业务提供最好的开发世界。

让我们开始使用最适合开发人员的 Vue 开发工具。 这些 VueJS 的开发者工具真的很有用。 因此，请密切关注这些顶级 VueJS 开发工具。

官方 Vue.Js 指南
还包括详尽的学习指南、Vue.js API 文档、官方编码风格指南、代码示例等。

故事书
它是一个 UI 开发和测试环境，支持 Vue、React、React Native、Angular 和 Ember 以及其他 JavaScript 框架。 故事是组件的单个状态的表示（如可视化测试用例）。 一个组件可以有很多故事，而一个Storybook通常有很多故事。

资料来源：故事书

该工具甚至支持隔离环境。 此外，Storybook 将使开发人员能够在主项目之外构建组件。 始终可以选择聘请专门的开发人员来满足您的 Web 开发要求，以便在不浪费宝贵时间和资金的情况下，一开始就做好。

元素
它带有大量可定制的 Vue 组件，您可以使用它们来创建具有专业外观的 Web 应用程序。 另一方面，Element UI 拥有庞大的社区并且正在积极开发，每天都有新的变化被推送。 您还可以使用由 Vue CLI 构建的 Element UI 启动工具包来快速入门。

来源：元素

您可以使用这些组件构建 Web 应用程序，而不会影响您的任何想法。 您还可以使用 Element UI 组件和功能来精确地按照您的设想构建 Web 应用程序。 首先，您可以使用 Element UI 启动工具包，然后升级到由 Vue CLI 创建的完整工具包。 对于 VueJS 开发者来说，这个工具包可能被认为是顶级的 Vue 开发工具之一。

特征：

  • 交互的
  • 可定制的主题
  • 可下载资源以提高效率

设置 VueJS 后，构建应用程序的第一步是放置一个合适的样板，以防止从头开始编写代码。 有多种样板开发替代方案，但 NuxtJS 允许您创建单页应用程序 (SPA)、渐进式 Web 应用程序 (PWA)、服务器端渲染 (SSR) 和静态站点。

资料来源： NUXT

此外，Nuxt 的模块化架构和 50 多个模块可帮助您完成诸如集成 Google Analytics、获得 PWA 优势和生成站点地图等操作，从而加快了您的开发过程。 验证
卡片、轮播、按钮、图标、工具栏和其他设计元素都包含在 80 多个语义和可重用的 Vue 组件中。 它还包括预构建的项目脚手架，因此您只需一个命令即可开始构建 Vue.js 应用程序。

资料来源： Vuetify

特征：

  • 具有自动摇树功能的更小的捆绑包
  • 庞大的 Discord 社区
  • 为主要版本提供 18 个月的长期支持
  • 具有 SASS/SCSS 和预设的广泛自定义选项
  • 响应式支持低至 320 像素宽度

材料
此外，考虑到最高的行业标准，为用户提供了最好的 VueJS 管理模板之一，它不仅使用快速简单，而且具有高度可扩展性。 凭借最大的易用性和多功能性，您将能够毫不费力地创建您想要的任何应用程序。 你也可以查看使用 VueJS 构建的 Laravel 管理模板。

资料来源： Materio

模糊搜索、深色、半深色和有边框的布局选择、高级卡片和图表只是其中一些有趣且有价值的功能。 它还具有一些出色的功能，例如下面列出的功能。

特征：

  • 纯 Vue js，无 Jquery 依赖
  • 使用 Vue CLI 创建
  • 使用 Vuex、Vue 路由器、Webpack
  • 代码拆分，延迟加载
  • 智威汤逊认证
  • 访问控制 (ACL)
  • 多语言支持
  • 它分为两个部分：

    来源： ViteJs

    具有高于原生 ES 模块的多项功能的开发服务器，例如高速热模块更换 (HMR)。 一个构建命令，它使用 Rollup 捆绑您的代码并将其设置为生成高度优化的静态资产以进行部署。

    特征：

    • 热模块更换
    • 打字稿
    • 静态资产
    • 它允许您快速开发项目结构和样板。 它还允许您创建快速原型来演示新功能。 除了典型的花里胡哨外，Vue CLI 还为 Babel、ESLint、TypeScript、PostCSS、PWA、单元测试和端到端测试等 Web 开发工具提供了更多支持。 该工具还与社区创建的可以扩展的第三方插件兼容。

      来源： Vue Cli

      此外，Vue CLI 不需要弹出。 与其他框架相比，Vue CLI 提供了更高级别的灵活性。 它是最好的 Vue 开发工具之一，因为它充当所有应用程序组件的集中存储，其规则保证状态只能以可预测的方式更改。 由于与 Vue 的官方 devtools 插件集成，还可以使用零配置时间旅行调试和状态快照导出/导入等高级功能。

      资料来源： Vuex

      所有状态管理任务都集中在这个 vuejs 开发工具中，状态只能以可预测的方式进行更改。 此外，允许根据状态、视图和操作进行更改。 此外，状态指定了用户通过视图看到的应用程序的真实性，并且动作导致状态发生变化，从而开始了突变周期。 视图
      另一个值得考虑的优秀 Vue UI 库是 View UI。 它包含大量有用且有吸引力的 Vue 组件。 它还将帮助您创建出色的 Web 应用程序。 它也是一个高质量的 Vue UI 组件库，包含数百个有用且美观的组件。 因此，它很容易上手，您甚至可以直观地开发新项目。

      资料来源： iView

      特征：

      • 数十个有用且美观的组件。
      • 友好的 API。 它适用于所有技能水平的个人。
      • 广泛的文档和演示。
      • 它很漂亮。

      用于性能测试的 Vue Performance DevTool
      性能测试是一种测量和识别所有上述性能瓶颈的方法。 这个插件的目的是检查 Vue 组件的性能。

      资料来源：开发工具

      此外，它使用 Vue 的“window.performance”API 对 Vue 组件的性能进行统计分析。 以下是 Vue Performance DevTools 的几个广泛应用：

      • 移除或卸载任何未使用的组件实例。
      • 检查导致操作停止或操作开始后需要更长时间的原因。
      • 检查加载时间最长的组件。

      本机脚本
      对于丰富的移动编程，该框架目前包括 iOS 和 Android 运行时。 它也可以用于各种不同的用途。

      类星体
      作为 Web 开发人员，这使您能够快速设计各种样式的响应式网站/应用程序。

      来源：类星体

      它可以通过 CSS 和 JS HTML 元素添加到当前项目中。 它还内置了对 SSR（服务器端渲染应用程序）的支持。 因此，它是最强烈推荐的 vue devtools 之一。

      特征：

      • 以性能为中心的框架
      • 通过 Cordova 或 Capacitor 的移动应用程序（Android、iOS）

      因此，如果您要进行 Vue 开发，我们建议您首先列出您的需求，然后选择最适合您项目需求的 Vue Devtools。 如果您正在处理需要专门前端开发人员的项目，我们建议您与专业的自定义 Web 应用程序开发公司合作。 专业的组织将采用最新的 VueJs 技术为您提供最佳结果。

原文:

Vue is a versatile technology that can be used to build applications without requiring a lot of resources. It is a progressive JavaScript framework used for designing any user interface, with 195k stars on GitHub.

Vue is extremely customizable when compared to other frameworks on the market. This implies you don’t have to fully comprehend all of the principles. It has received a lot of attention from programmers in a short space of time.

Vue framework is used for creating progressive user interfaces. It is built from the ground up and can function as both a library and a framework, depending on your needs. It is made up of a view layer-focused core library and an ecosystem of supplementary libraries. Evan You is the creator of Vue. Vue is a progressive JavaScript framework that is a faster alternative to Angular. VueJS, unlike Angular and React, is not funded by major behemoths such as Facebook and Google. Despite this, VueJS has become the most popular js framework because to its numerous features and benefits.

It is relatively compact, lightweight, and simple to use when compared to more complicated front-end frameworks such as Angular, Backbone, and React. Furthermore, Vue.js is gaining popularity among developers at a rapid pace.

Gitlab, Alibaba, Adobe, Xiaomi, Euronews, Behance, Nintendo, Grammarly, Codeship, and many others are among the brands that use VueJs.

Furthermore, Vue is trusted by the majority of well-known brands for their online apps. As a result, it could be a good strategy for you to use it for your business. A custom web application development company is equipped with the necessary skill sets to provide the best of the development world for your business.

Let’s get started with the Best Vue Devtools for Developers. These developer tools for VueJS are really useful. So, keep an eye on these top VueJS devtools.

Official Vue.Js Guide
The official Vue.js tutorial will assist you in getting started with the framework. A thorough learning guide, the Vue.js API documentation, the official coding style guide, code samples, and more are also included.

Storybook
Storybook is the top in our list of the greatest vue devtools. It’s a UI development and testing environment which supports Vue, React, React Native, Angular, and Ember, among other JavaScript frameworks. Storybook allows you to develop your own component library in the form of a series of stories. A story is a representation of a single state of a component (like a visual test case). A component can have a lot of stories, and a Storybook generally has a lot of them.

Source: Storybook

The Vue tool will make it simple for developers to create and test beautiful UI components. Even the isolated environment is supported by the tool. Furthermore, Storybook will enable developers to construct components apart from the main project. There is always the option to hire dedicated developers for your web development requirements, to get it right on the first go without wasting precious time and funds.

Element
Element UI is one of the most popular Vue.js 2.0 UI toolkits at the moment. It comes with a plethora of customizable Vue components that you can use to create a professional-looking web app. Element UI, on the other hand, has a large community and is actively developed, with new changes being pushed every day. You may also utilize Element UI’s starting kit, which was built by Vue CLI, to get started quickly.

Source: Element

You can build a web application using these components without compromising any of your ideas. You can also use Element UI components and functionalities to construct the web application precisely how you envisioned it. To start, you can utilize the Element UI starting kit, and then upgrade to the full toolkit created by Vue CLI later. For VueJS developers, this toolkit might be regarded one of the top Vue devtools.

Features:

  • Interactive
  • Customizable themes
  • Downloadable resources to increase efficiency

NuxtJS
NuxtJS is a Vue framework that is simple to use. After you’ve set up VueJS, the first step in constructing your apps is to put up a suitable boilerplate that allows you to prevent writing code from start. There are a variety of boilerplate development alternatives, but NuxtJS allows you to create Single Page Applications (SPAs), Progressive Web Applications (PWAs), Server-Side Rendered (SSRs), and Static Sites.

Source: NUXT

Furthermore, Nuxt speeds up your development process because of its modular architecture and over 50 modules which help you do things like integrate Google Analytics, get PWA advantages, and generate a sitemap. Nuxt’s bundle analyzer also supports VueJS and NodeJS best practices and provides out-of-the-box performance tweaking.

Vuetify
Vuetify is a well-known Vue.js component framework that adheres to the Material Design standards set out by Google. Cards, carousels, buttons, icons, toolbars, and other design elements are among the 80+ semantic and reusable Vue components included. Vuetify works with all current web browsers. It also includes pre-built project scaffolding so you can get started constructing your Vue.js app with only one command.

Source: Vuetify

Features:

  • WCAG Accessibility (a11y) / WCAG / Section 508 / RTL support
  • 30+ supported languages
  • Smaller bundle sizes with automatic tree-shaking
  • A massive Discord community
  • 18 months of Long-term support for Major releases
  • Extensive customization options with SASS/SCSS and Presets
  • Responsive support down to 320px width

Materio
Materio Vuetify Vuejs Admin Dashboard Template — is the latest Vuetify-based Admin Dashboard Template that is both developer-friendly and extremely customizable. Furthermore, the highest industry standards are taken into account to deliver users one of the best VueJS admin templates that are not only quick and simple to use, but also highly scalable. With maximum ease and versatility, you’ll be able to create any application you desire with no effort. You may also check out at Laravel Admin Templates built with VueJS.

Source: Materio

Materio also has a number of useful features aimed at assisting you in creating high-quality single-page apps precisely as you want them. Fuzzy search, Dark, Semi-Dark, and Bordered layout choices, Advanced cards, and Charts are just a few of the interesting and valuable features. It also has several fantastic features, such as the ones listed below.

Features:

  • Pure Vue js, No Jquery Dependency
  • Created with Vue CLI
  • Utilizes Vuex, Vue Router, Webpack
  • Code Splitting, Lazy loading
  • JWT Authentication
  • Access Control (ACL)
  • i18n Page
  • Multi-lingual Support
  • 2 chart libraries
  • 3 Dashboard
  • SASS Powered and many more.

ViteJS
ViteJS is a build tool for contemporary web projects that attempts to make development easier and faster. Vite is likewise uncompromising and ships with good defaults out of the box, but it’s also very extendable because because of its Plugin API and JavaScript API, both of which have complete type support. It is divided into two sections:

Source: ViteJs

A development server with several features above native ES modules, such as highly fast Hot Module Replacement (HMR). A build command which bundles your code using Rollup and sets it up to produce highly optimized static assets for deployment.

Features:

  • NPM Dependency Resolving and Pre-Bundling
  • Hot Module Replacement
  • TypeScript
  • Vue
  • JSX
  • CSS
  • Static Assets
  • JSON

Vue-CLI
Vue-CLI is a set of tools for working with VueJS. It allows you to quickly develop project structures and boilerplates. It also allows you to create rapid prototypes to demonstrate new features. In addition to the typical bells and whistles, the Vue CLI has a lot more support for web development tools like Babel, ESLint, TypeScript, PostCSS, PWA, Unit Testing, and End-to-end Testing. The tool is also compatible with community-created third-party plugins that may be extended.

Source: Vue Cli

Furthermore, ejecting is not required with Vue CLI. In comparison to other frameworks, the Vue CLI allows for a greater level of flexibility. Vue CLI’s Graphical User Interface (GUI) is one of the most useful features, allowing you to easily create new projects and manage them.

Vuex
Vuex is a Vue.js application state management paradigm and library. It’s one of the finest Vue devtools since it acts as a centralized store for all of an application’s components, with rules guaranteeing that the state may only be changed in a predictable way. Advanced capabilities like zero-config time-travel debugging and state snapshot export/import are also available thanks to integration with Vue’s official devtools plugin.

Source: Vuex

All state management tasks are centralized in this vuejs development tool, and states may only be altered in a predictable manner. Furthermore, mutations depending on State, View, and Actions are permitted. Furthermore, the state specifies the application’s truth, which the user sees through the view, and actions cause the state to change, beginning the mutation cycle. Vuex additionally includes functions such as state snapshot export/import and zero-config time-travel debugging.

IView
Another excellent Vue UI library to consider is View UI. It includes a plethora of useful and attractive Vue components. It will also assist you in creating a fantastic web application. It’s also a high-quality Vue UI component library that includes hundreds of useful and beautiful components. As a result, it’s simple to get started with, and you can even develop new projects visually.

Source: iView

Features:

  • Dozens of useful and beautiful components.
  • Friendly API. It is appropriate for individuals of all skill levels.
  • Extensive documentation and demos.
  • It is quite beautiful.

Vue Performance DevTool for Performance Testing
Performance testing is a method of measuring and identifying all of the aforementioned performance bottlenecks. Vue Performance DevTools is a browser plugin that improves the performance of your website. The purpose of this plugin is to examine the performance of Vue components.

Source: Devtools

Furthermore, it does a statistical analysis of a Vue component’s performance using Vue’s ‘window.performance’ API. The following are a couple of wide range of applications of Vue Performance DevTools:

  • Remove or unmount any component instances that aren’t in use.
  • Examine the reasons that cause an operation to stop or take longer once it has begun.
  • Examine the component that takes the longest to load.

Nativescript
NativeScript allows you to use native APIs straight from JavaScript. For rich mobile programming, the framework presently includes iOS and Android runtimes. It can also be used for a variety of different uses.

Quasar
Quasar is a high-performance Material Design component package with extensions for SPAs, SSRs, PWAs, Mobile (Cordova & Capacitor), Desktop (Electron), and Browsers. As a web developer, this enables you to swiftly design responsive websites/apps in a variety of styles.

Source: Quasar

A UMD (Unified Module Definition) version is also available from Quasar. It can be added to the current project via CSS and JS HTML elements. It also has built-in support for SSR (Server-side Rendered App). As a result, it is one of the most highly recommended vue devtools.

Features:

  • Performance-focused framework
  • PWAs (Progressive Web App)
  • BEX (Browser Extension)
  • Mobile Apps (Android, iOS) through Cordova or Capacitor

So, if you’re going to be doing Vue development, we recommend making a list of your requirements first, and then picking the best Vue Devtools that fit your project’s demands. We recommend working with a professional custom web application development company if you’re working on a project that requires dedicated frontend developers. A professional organization will employ the most up-to-date VueJs technology to provide you with the best results.

Items.Title: JavaScript 终极指南 02：DOM | JavaScript Ultimate Guide 02: The DOM
Items.Description:
作为 Web 开发人员，如果您想创建动态网页，则必须通过 DOM 来完成。

在本文中，我将展示 dom 是什么以及如何使用 DOM 操作网页。

表中的内容

先决条件

什么是 DOM？

先决条件

我假设您了解 JavaScript 的基础知识。
如果您还不了解基础知识，请不要担心。 我写了一篇涵盖所有JS 基础主题的文章。 你可以在这里阅读。

所以，事不宜迟，让我们开始吧。

什么是 DOM？

这是 MDN 对 DOM 的评价。

文档对象模型 (DOM) 是Web 文档的编程接口
它表示页面，以便程序可以更改文档结构、样式和内容。

通过使用 JavaScript 操作 DOM，您可以为网页添加交互性。

让我们看看如何使用 DOM 操作网页。

使用 DOM 选择单个元素

要首先操作网页，您需要访问其元素。 使用 DOM，您可以通过多种方式访问​​单个元素。 这里有几个：

  1. 使用 DOM 选择多个元素

    使用 DOM 时，有时您需要一次选择多个元素。 有很多方法可以一次获取多个元素。

    像：

    1. 使用 DOM 为元素设置样式

      现在，您知道如何选择单个元素和多个元素。

      让我们为您选择的元素添加一些样式。

      您可以使用 .style 属性和要添加或更改的 CSS 属性为元素添加样式。

      这是一个例子。

       const body = document . quearySelector ( " body " ); body . style . color = " red " ;

      使用 addEventListener 添加事件

      在我们给元素添加事件之前，首先你需要了解什么是事件？

      事件是用户或浏览器做某事时发生的某种动作。

      以下是一些事件示例：

      • 浏览器加载网站
      • 用户更改输入字段
      • 用户点击了一个按钮
      • 用户将鼠标悬停在元素上。

      最常用的事件之一是 OnClick 事件。 这是为元素的单击事件添加的。

      这是一个点击事件的例子。

       const btn = document . querySelector ( " .btn " ); btn . addEventLister ( " click " , function () { console . log ( " The button was clicked " ); })

      要添加事件，首先，您需要使用任何 DOM 选择器抓取元素。

      然后，您必须向该元素添加.addEventListener("event", eventHandlerFunction)方法。

      如您所见， addEventLister 方法有 2 个参数，一个是事件，另一个是事件处理函数。

      事件处理函数确定事件发生时会发生什么。

      在我们上面的示例中，当您单击按钮时，它将在 console.log 中显示“按钮被单击”字符串。

      结论。

      在本文中，我们讨论了 DOM 是什么以及如何使用 DOM 查询 HTML 元素，以及最后如何向它们添加 EventLIsenter。

      感谢您阅读到最后。 如果你喜欢这篇文章，你可能也会喜欢我的其他内容。

      在 Twitter @coderamrin上关注我以查看我的所有内容。



原文:

DOM is one of the most important concepts for web developers. As a web developer, if you want to create a dynamic web page, you’ll have to do that through DOM.

In this article, I’ll show what dom is and how to manipulate a web page with DOM.

Table Of Content

Prerequisite

What is DOM?

Selecting Single Element with DOM

Selecting multiple elements with DOM

Styling an Element with DOM

Adding Events with addEventListener

Prerequisite

I assume you know the fundamentals of JavaScript.
If you don’t know the fundamentals yet, don’t worry. I wrote an article that covers all the JS fundamental topics. You can read it here.

So, without any delay, let's get started.

What is DOM?

DOM stands for Document Object Model.

Here’s what MDN says about DOM.

The Document Object Model (DOM) is a programming interface for web documents
. It represents the page so that programs can change the document structure, style, and content.

By manipulating the DOM with JavaScript you can add interactivity to the web page.

Let’s look at how you can manipulate the web page with DOM.

Selecting Single Element with DOM

To manipulate a web page first you need to access its elements. With DOM you can access a single element in many ways. Here are a few:

  1. getElementById(”elements-id”)
  2. querySelector(”element name or class, or id”) 

//get element by ID
const element = document.getElementById("intro"); //Get element with querySelector
const elementQuery = document.querySelector("intro");

Selecting multiple elements with DOM

When working with DOM sometimes you’ll need to select multiple elements at once. There are quite a few ways to get multiple elements at once.

Like:

  1. getElementsByClassName(”Id”);
  2. querySelectorAll(”intro”) 
 //get all elements by class names
const element = document.getElementsByClassName("intro"); //Get all elements with querySelector
const elementQuery = document.querySelector("intro");

Styling an Element with DOM

Now that, you know how to select single element and multiple elements.

Let's add some style to the element you selected.

You can add styling to your element with the .style property and the CSS property you want to add or change.

Here’s an example.


const body = document.quearySelector("body");
body.style.color = "red";

Adding Events with addEventListener

Before we add events to elements, first you need to understand what an event is?

Events are some kind of action that happens when the user or browser does something.

Here are a few examples of events:

  • Browser load the website
  • User changes an input field
  • User clicked on a button
  • User hovers over an element.

One of the most used events is the OnClick event. Which is added for the click event of an element.

here's an example of a click event.


const btn = document.querySelector(".btn"); btn.addEventLister("click", function() { console.log("The button was clicked");
})

To add the event, first, you’ll need to grab the element with any DOM selector.

Then you’ll have to add .addEventListener("event", eventHandlerFunction) method to that element.

As you can see addEventLister method takes 2 arguments, one is the event and the other one is the event handler function.

The event handler function determines what will happen when the event occurs.

In our example above, when you clicked the button it will console.log "The button was clicked" string.

Conclusion.

In this article, we discussed what DOM is and how to query HTML elements with DOM, and finally how to add EventLIstenter to them.

Thanks for reading till the end. If you enjoyed this article you might enjoy my other content too.

Follow me on Twitter @coderamrin to see all my stuff.

Items.Title: 在 Java 中插入 PDF 中的图像 | Insert Images in PDF in Java
Items.Description:
图片是PDF文档的重要组成部分，在PDF页面的适当位置插入图片可以使文档更具吸引力。 本文将分享如何使用 Free Spire.PDF for Java 将图像插入到现有的 PDF 文档中。

导入Jar依赖（2种方法）

方法一：下载免费库并解压。 然后将 Spire.Pdf.jar 文件作为依赖项添加到您的 Java 应用程序中。
方法二：在pom.xml文件中添加如下配置，直接将jar依赖添加到maven项目中。

 <repository> <id>com.e-iceblue</id> <name>e-iceblue</name> <url>https://repo.e-iceblue.com/nexus/content/groups/public/</url> </repository> </repositories> <dependencies> <dependency> <groupId>e-iceblue</groupId> <artifactId>spire.pdf.free</artifactId> <version>5.1.0</version> </dependency> </dependencies>

示例代码

以下代码显示了如何将图像插入到现有的 PDF 文档中，为了避免覆盖文本内容，我将透明度设置为插入的图像。

 import com.spire.pdf.* ; import com.spire.pdf.graphics.PdfImage ; public class InsertImage { public static void main ( String [] args ) { //Create PDF document PdfDocument pdf = new PdfDocument (); //Load the PDF document from disk pdf . loadFromFile ( "D:\\Files\\input.pdf" ); //Get a specified page PdfPageBase page = pdf . getPages (). get ( 0 ); //Load an image PdfImage image = PdfImage . fromFile ( "C:\\Users\\Administrator\\Desktop\\Olympics.jpg" ); //Set the width and height of image float width = image . getWidth () * 0.75f ; float height = image . getHeight () * 0.75f ; //Define a position to draw image double x = ( page . getCanvas (). getClientSize (). getWidth ()- width ) / 2 ; float y = 300 f ; //Set image transparency page . getCanvas (). setTransparency ( 0.5f ); //Draw image on page canvas page . getCanvas (). drawImage ( image , x , y , width , height ); //Save the document pdf . saveToFile ( "insertImage.pdf" , FileFormat . PDF ); pdf . close (); } }



原文:

Image is an important part of PDF documents, and inserting images at appropriate positions on a PDF page can make the document more attractive. This article will share how to insert an image into an exixting PDF document using Free Spire.PDF for Java.

Import Jar Dependency (2 Methods)

Method 1: Download the free library and unzip it. Then add the Spire.Pdf.jar file to your Java application as dependency.
Method 2: Directly add the jar dependency to maven project by adding the following configurations to the pom.xml file.

    <repository>
        <id>com.e-iceblue</id>
        <name>e-iceblue</name>
        <url>https://repo.e-iceblue.com/nexus/content/groups/public/</url>
    </repository>
</repositories>
<dependencies>
    <dependency>
        <groupId>e-iceblue</groupId>
        <artifactId>spire.pdf.free</artifactId>
        <version>5.1.0</version>
    </dependency>
</dependencies>

Sample Code

Free Spire.PDF for Java provides the page.getCanvas().drawImage() method to add images to the PDFs when creating as well as insert images into existing PDFs. The following code shows how to insert an image into an exixting PDF document, and to avoid covering the text content, I set the transparency to the inserted image.

import com.spire.pdf.*;
import com.spire.pdf.graphics.PdfImage; public class InsertImage { public static void main(String[] args) { //Create PDF document PdfDocument pdf = new PdfDocument(); //Load the PDF document from disk pdf.loadFromFile("D:\\Files\\input.pdf"); //Get a specified page PdfPageBase page = pdf.getPages().get(0); //Load an image PdfImage image = PdfImage.fromFile("C:\\Users\\Administrator\\Desktop\\Olympics.jpg"); //Set the width and height of image float width = image.getWidth() * 0.75f; float height = image.getHeight() * 0.75f; //Define a position to draw image double x = (page.getCanvas().getClientSize().getWidth()-width) /2; float y = 300f; //Set image transparency page.getCanvas().setTransparency(0.5f); //Draw image on page canvas page.getCanvas().drawImage(image, x, y, width, height); //Save the document pdf.saveToFile("insertImage.pdf", FileFormat.PDF); pdf.close(); }
}
Items.Author: carlwils

Items.Title: 基础设施即代码 (IaC) 这一切是如何开始的 - 1. 部分 | Infrastructure as Code (IaC) how it all started - 1. part
Items.Description:
拉多万·马科夫斯基

发表于大家好，所有对基础架构即代码 (IaC) 感兴趣的人。
本文适用于任何想了解更多有关 IaC 及其演变的信息的人。 它更多的是关于从过去到现在的旅程。 这完全取决于我的个人经验和偏好。

感叹号！！！

但为什么？ 以免先回顾过去。

故事

早在九十年代，我就以 Linux 管理员的身份开始了我的运营商。 那时的 Linux 非常新鲜，但已经足够成熟，可以处理这项工作。 还有 Windows 服务器系列，包括 Windows NT 服务器和 Windows 2000。
我一直用 Debian，现在不记得确切的版本了，但这在我们的故事中并不重要。 我对这两种环境都有合理的相似开始。 我同时使用了几年，效果很好。 每个系统都有其优缺点。 为什么 Windows 不能成为 IaC 的专业解决方案？ 从一开始就是有缺陷的。 如果是的话，那是最强和最弱的部分。 您可以使用 GUI 完成所有操作。 那挺好的。 但是你能复制它吗？ 如果您使用的是英文版，则基本没问题。 但是，当您使用一些本地化时，乐趣就开始了。 在我的时代，它是捷克语。 然后它统计到非常令人困惑。 翻译是问题的一部分。 如果您知道英语术语，它并不总是按您期望的那样翻译。 另一个问题是排序。 菜单中图标的排序完全不同。 因此，如果您还记得图标是按某种顺序排序的。 在不同的语言版本中，它们不是。 也经常没有像 Linux 那样简单的切换语言的方法。 有许可证的东西:(。如果你买了捷克语，那么你就不能使用英语，如果不是简单的开关，比如在 env 中定义一些变量。另一个乐趣来自于较新的版本。他们倾向于重新发明一个轮子, 稍微改组了一些东西，在这个过程中制造了更多的混乱。Windows 下的罢工。我停止使用它...... Linux 也有 GUI，但主要关注的是命令行。一切都可以在命令行中完成。并且有我喜欢的那个结构。一切都准备好了。毕竟有一些差异，但即使是不同的发行版也总是在 /etc 中配置，在 /var/log 中登录等等。所以只需很少的 grepping，你就可以有效地使用任何发行版和任何版本。

我不再使用 Windows 服务器，我不打算这样做。 我知道命令行界面有一些演变，但我不想再探索它了。 好吧，我最近正在使用一些 Windows 服务器。 但它纯粹的痛苦，痛苦，痛苦。

所以它是Linux，这一切都始于| 您可以编写一个简单的衬线来做有趣的事情，然后您可以编写一个简单的 bash 脚本来做另一件更好的事情。 但
这一切都导致了脚本的噩梦。 所有服务器都充满了脚本。 各种版本。 只需数千个脚本。 如果您的环境很大，则很难维护。 到那时，所有的工作都是手动的。 连接到服务器，运行一些脚本，更改，运行并再次。 如果你必须管理数千台机器，那是很困难的。 有一些堡垒或 jumphost 服务器，您可以从其中运行这些脚本，从 cron 或手动或通过某些网页。 为这些脚本提供变量很困难，而且还有很多问题。 这些脚本的描述在 90% 中缺失，同样适用于可以提供的参数的描述。 很明显，这不是办法。 有些事情必须改变。

木偶 - 厨师 - Ansible - SaltStack

当这些工具发布时，已经有一段时间了。 我在一定程度上都使用了它们。 对我来说，Ansible 显然是赢家。 但是让我们听听故事的原因。

推或拉

一开始就讨论了什么更好。 但过了一会儿，赢家就清楚了。 没有任何。 不要紧。 它始终与用例有关。 但是，您可以调整所有这些系统以使其行为与预期相反。

速度

关于速度总是一个很大的讨论。 但这更多的是关于你如何编写它，或者你的方法是否好。 但我最看重的是易用性和结果的稳定性。 速度比赛将赢得 Saltstack 或厨师，也许是木偶。 但对我来说，速度是最不感兴趣的指标。 如果需要很长时间。 反正你做错了什么:-)

代理或无代理

理论上代理赢了。 在实践中它不是那么清楚。 问题很少。

  • 代理升级 - 在过去，升级到新的主要版本是一件事情。 有鸡和蛋的问题。 您有旧版本的代理，无法使用新代码。 并且您无法升级到新版本的代理，因为它不在旧系统上运行。 唯一的方法是一次升级所有系统以及代码库。 或者使用不同版本的代码创建两个服务器。 或者我根本不升级并坚持使用旧版本。 总是同步 - 你必须有好的金丝雀主机来测试你的更改，或者当你想测试一些困难的东西时，你必须做一些技巧来停止下载代码。 我碰巧犯了一个错误，弄乱了所有服务器上的一点防火墙配置，所以它们都被拒绝了。 好玩。 至少他们有出口工作并且能够下载固定版本。

  • 卡住 - 这些代理经常无缘无故地卡住。 我不得不杀了他们很多次。 即使服务器上没有其他问题。

安装 ssh 和 Python 就足够了，一切顺利。 没有卡住的代理。 不需要金丝雀主机，升级也没有那么大的麻烦。 您必须解决如何在整个基础架构上运行，但现在很容易。 我从未使用过那个 AnibleTower。 复杂性和特点

这些工具之间有大量的比较文章。 我会简单地在 Ansible 上写我喜欢的，而在其他人上不喜欢。
当然，木偶、厨师和盐是非常强大的工具。 毫无疑问。 但这是有代价的。 而且价格很高。 如果您不维持秩序，您将射中自己的腿。 例如，很难获得将应用于服务器的检查。 你永远不知道取了哪些变量，这非常令人困惑。 你必须运行它并祈祷。 一旦我在非常意想不到的地方发现一些厨师代码中的一些变量发生了变化。 我像。 我勒个去。 同样的事情也适用于盐。 我能够阅读它在做什么，但它更像任何其他编程语言。 任何地方都没有评论。 问题是您了解它在做什么，但您不知道编写它的程序员的意图是什么。 我们都知道这些系统可能非常复杂。 使用 Ansible，每段代码都以name开头，即使是大多数懒惰的管理员也倾向于在那里填写至少一些信息，这对未来非常有帮助。
通常需要运行一个计时器。 您可能想升级或只做一次事情。 有了这些代理工具，就不是很简单了。 您必须拥有用于该工作的其他工具。 那很不方便。 也许这一次它改变了，但我怀疑。 使用 Ansible，您可以为新编写的一个计时器作业使用相同的清单、相同的变量、相同的一切。 恢复

所以毕竟，Ansible 是明显的赢家。 它易于调试，易于测试，易于运行，易于理解其他管理员的工作，非常灵活，可用于一个计时器或重复运行的作业。

第一部分结束。

在评论区留言。

Dipanshu Torawane - Dipanshu Torawane - Mar 28 3月28日



原文:

Radovan Makovsky

Posted on Apr 27

Hello all interested in Infrastructure as Code (IaC).
This article is for anybody who whats to learn little bit more about the IaC and how it evolved. It's more about the journey from past towards the present. It's all about my personal experience and preference.

IaC is a must, there is no exception. !!! Exclamation Mark !!!

But why? Lest take a look to the past first.

The story

I started my carrier as Linux admin some way back in nineties. The Linux was very fresh by that time, but mature enough to handle the work. There was also the Windows server family with Windows NT server and Windows 2000.
I used always Debian, do not remember now the exact version, but it does not matter in our story. I had reasonable similar start with both environments. Windows and Linux. I used them for several years in parallel with good results. Each of the systems had it cons and pros. Windows was better for managing users accounts with Active Domain (AD) and Linux was better in everything else.

Windows vs Linux

Why Windows cannot be professional solution for IaC? It's flawed from start. The Windows ecosystem is focused on GUI. That is the strongest and weakest part if it. You can do everything with GUI. That's good. But can you replicate it? If you are using English version, you are mostly OK. But fun starts when you use some localization. In my times it was Czech. Then it stat to be very confusing all. The translation is the part of the issue. If you know the English term, it not always translated as you expect. The other issue is with the sorting. The sorting of icons in the menu is simply different. So if you remember that that icons were sorted in some order. In Different language version they are not. Also often there was not simple way of switching language as in the Linux. Something with the licensing :(. If you bought Czech, then you could not use English, and if it was not simple switch like defining few variables in env. The other fun comes with newer versions. They tend to invent a wheel all over again, reshuffling thing little bit, creating more chaos in the process. Strike down for Windows. I stopped using it... The Linux had also GUI, but primary focus was for command-line. Everything could be done in command-line. And there was that structure i liked. Everything was in place. There were some differences after all, but even different distributions had always configuration in /etc, logs in /var/log and so on. So with little grepping you could work efficiently with any distribution and any version.

I do not use Windows servers any more, i do not plan to. I know there was some evolvement regarding the command-line interface, but i do not want to explore it anymore. Well I was using some Windows servers recently. But its pure pain, pain, pain.

Linux

So it was the Linux and it all started with |. You could write simple one liners to do interesting stuff and then you could write a simple bash script to do another better stuff. BUT
It all resulted in scripts nightmare. All servers were full of scripts. Various versions. Simply thousands of scripts. If you had large environment it was very hard to maintain. By that time all work was manual. Connect to server, run some script, change, run and again. If you had to manage several thousands of machines, it was difficult. There were some bastions or jumphost server from where you could run these scripts, from cron, or by hand or by some web page. It was difficult to provide variables for these scripts and it had many more issues. The description of these scripts were missing in 90% the same applies for description of parameters which can be supplied. It was obvious that this is not the way. Something had to change.

Puppet - Chef - Ansible - SaltStack

2005 - 2009 - 2012 - 2011

Its already quite a while, when these tools were released. I used them all for some extend. For me is the Ansible clear winner. Buts let hear the story why.

Push or Pull

It was quite a discussion at the beginning what is better. But after a while the winner was clear. NONE. It does not matter. It's always about use case. But these all these systems you can tune up to behave in opposite they were intended for.

Speed

It's always a big discussion about speed. But it is more about how you write it, or if your approach is good. But what i value most is ease of usage and stability of results. The speed contest would win Saltstack or chef, maybe puppet. But for me the speed is the least interesting metrics. If it takes long. You are doing something wrong anyway :-)

Agent or no Agent

In theory the agent wins. In practice its not so clear. There are few problems.

  • upgrades of agents - In these old days, upgrading to a new major version was a thing. There was chicken and egg problem. You had old version of agents, which wont work with new code. and you could not upgrade to the new version of agent as it was not running on the old system. The only way was to upgrade all the systems at once and also the code base. Or create two servers with different versions of code. Or i don't upgrade at all and stick with the old version. :-) Clear winner OLD version.

  • always syncing - You had to have good canary hosts to test your changes, or do some tricks to stop downloading the code all the time when you wanted to test something difficult. It happened to me that i made a mistake and messed up little bit firewall configuration on all servers so they had all ingress DENY. It was fun. At least they had egress working and were able to download fixed version.

  • stuck - These agents were stuck so often for no reason. I had to kill them so many times. Even there were no other issues on the servers.

Ansible does not have agent. It's enough to have ssh and Python installed and you are good to go. No stuck agents. No need for canary hosts, and no such big troubles with upgrades. You have to resolve how to run in on whole infrastructure, but these days its easy. Pipeline in Gitlab CI or Jenkins or Argo or any other pipelining tools.
I never used that AnibleTower. It's too much GUI :-) Should I try?

Complexity and features

There is ton of comparison articles between those tools. I'll simply write what i like on Ansible and do no like on others.
Certainly puppet, chef and salt are very powerful tools. No doubt about that. But there is price for that. And the price is high. If you do not maintain order you will shoot yourself in the leg. As example it was very difficult to get what check will apply to the server. You never know which variables was taken, it was very confusing. you had to run it and pray. Once i found some variable changed inside some chef code on the very unexpected place. I was like. What the hell. The same thing applies to salt. I was able to read what it is doing, but it was more like any other programming language. No comments ever anywhere. The problems is you understand what what it is doing, but you don't know what was intention of that programmer who wrote it. And we all know that these systems can be extremely complex. With Ansible every piece of code starts with name Even most lazy admins tent to fill up at least some information there, which can be very helpfull in the future.
Often there is a need to run one timer. It can happen that you want to upgrade or simply do one time thing. With these agent tools, its not very simple. You had to have some other tool which you used for that job. That was inconvenient. Maybe these time it changed, but i doubt. With Ansible you can use the same inventory, the same variables, the same everything, for your freshly written one timer job. :-)

Resume

So after all, the Ansible is the clear winner. Its easy to debug, its easy to test, its easy to run, its easy to understand other admins work, It's very flexible and can be used to one timers or for repeatedly running jobs.

First part end.

PS: What is your experience? Leave message in the comment section.

Dipanshu Torawane - Mar 28
Items.Author: nfp007

Items.Title: 我的第一个太空服务 | My first Space service
Items.Description:
任何人都需要时不时地做新的事情。 今天我决定同时做两件新的事情。 将我的第一篇文章写到 DEV.to 并正式成为我的第一个 Space 应用程序。

动机

自 2019 年发布第一个预览版以来，我们一直在团队中使用它（发布时我在 KotlinConf，非常兴奋）。 现在，我们正在将大部分工作从 Slack/Confluence/GitHub 堆栈转移到带有 GitHub 镜像的 Space 中。

对于我们的团队来说，知识库非常重要，我们期待在 Space 中使用它。 我们停止使用 Confluence，但没有成熟的替代品。 我们开始使用 YouTrack 中的知识库，这还不错，但它仍然以某种方式扼杀了多合一工具的目的。

最近，Space 中的知识库（ Documents ）进入了下一个演化阶段，终于可以使用了。 我们开始在那里编写技术文档和注释。 但是发现，我们需要一种方法来以某种方式从太空中获取它。

目前，Space 不提供仅下载目录的方法（我认为该功能值得添加），因此我决定使用Space API创建一个应用程序来执行此操作。

执行

该代码可在repo中找到。 你可以从它开始。

我会试着引导你完成它。

阶段 1. 创建应用程序

首先，您需要创建一个 Space 应用程序来提供对 API 的访问。 转到Administration -> Applications并创建一个新的。 您需要像这里显示的那样设置权限。 您需要哪些权限并不总是很明显，错误消息有时会产生误导。 例如，我花了一些时间想知道为什么我得到一个正确请求的零响应只是为了发现，我需要手动为受限项目添加权限。 但我认为他们将来会清理它。

身份验证始终是最难的事情，因此我采用了默认方式（仅使用客户端 ID 和密码），但事实证明它非常容易。
您可以在同一部分的 Playground 中尝试 API，并深入了解它们的工作原理。

第 2 阶段。使用 Space SDK 设置项目。

只需将我的构建文件作为参考。

 repositories { mavenCentral () maven ( "https://maven.pkg.jetbrains.space/public/p/space/maven" ) } val ktorVersion = "1.6.4" dependencies { implementation ( "io.ktor:ktor-client-core:$ktorVersion" ) implementation ( "io.ktor:ktor-client-cio:$ktorVersion" ) implementation ( "io.ktor:ktor-client-auth:$ktorVersion" ) implementation ( "org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-cli:0.3.4" ) implementation ( "org.jetbrains:space-sdk-jvm:98244-beta" ) implementation ( "ch.qos.logback:logback-classic:1.2.10" ) testImplementation ( kotlin ( "test" )) }

请务必使用正确的 Ktor 版本。 对我来说2.0.0不起作用，因为 SDK 依赖于它。

你不需要其他任何东西。

第 3 阶段。建立连接。

这非常容易：

 val space : SpaceClient = SpaceClient ( ktorClientForSpace ( CIO ), SpaceAppInstance ( clientId ?: System . getProperty ( "space.clientId" ), clientSecret ?: System . getProperty ( "space.clientSecret" ), spaceUrl ), SpaceAuth . ClientCredentials () )

第 4 阶段。做逻辑。

整个逻辑都在 这个文件中。 你可以看到没有新的类（看，妈，我是一个函数式程序员！）。

因此，您只需获取客户端并查看可用的方法和属性。 像这样：

 projects . documents . folders . subfolders . listSubfolders ( projectId , folderId )

有时它不会按预期工作，因此您必须进行实验。 例如我必须这样做：

 val documents = projects . documents . folders . documents . listDocumentsInFolder ( projectId , folderId ) { id () } documents . data . forEach { val document = projects . documents . getDocument ( projectId , it . id ) { id () title () documentBody () bodyType () } downloadDocument ( directory , document ) }

因为listDocumentsInFolder返回没有正文的文档（这是合理的，但从 API 中并不明显）。

第 5 阶段。一个 hacky 部分。

文件下载相当顺利。 但是没有，我们有带有附件链接的降价文档：

  • 相对的，

  • 指向需要身份验证的 Internet 文件。

为了解决这个问题，我们需要下载这些图像并用本地文件引用替换链接。

目前，Space 不提供下载文件的 API（或者至少我没有找到），所以我不得不做一点逆向工程。

我检查了用于访问图像的请求，发现它们有一个额外的授权标头Bearer 。 同样，Space 客户端用于访问页面：

所以我只需要手动构建请求即可下载图像：

 val response = ktorClient . request < HttpResponse > { url ( "${server.serverUrl}/d/$imageId" ) method = HttpMethod . Get header ( HttpHeaders . Authorization , "Bearer ${token().accessToken}" ) }

这是一个 hack，文件的实际位置将来可能会改变，但它现在可以工作。

剩下的就是找到图像参考并用下载的降价文件中的文本替换它们。

就这样。

结论

我希望你会喜欢玩 Space API，我希望会有很多新的插件和应用程序来丰富生态系统。 我很喜欢它。 特别是与较旧的重量级系统相比。 奖金

由于我们不仅需要下载文档，还需要从中创建报告，这里是使用 PanDoc 将文档组合成报告的简单 PowerShell 脚本：



原文:

Anyone needs to do new things from time to time. Today I decided to do two new things simultaneously. Write my first article to DEV.to and make official my first Space application.

TL;DR - go for the code: https://github.com/altavir/space-document-extractor

Motivation

JetBrains Space is a relatively new fast-growing all-in-one solution for intranet/team workspace. We've been using it in our team since the first preview announcement in 2019 (I was in KotlinConf when it was announced and got pretty excited). Right now we are moving most of our work from Slack/Confluence/GitHub stack into Space with GitHub mirror.

For our team, the knowledge base is very important and we were looking forward to using it in Space. We stopped using Confluence, but there are no full-fledged alternatives. We started using the knowledge base in YouTrack, which is not bad, but it still somehow kills the purpose of the all-in-one tool.

Recently, the knowledge base ( Documents ) in Space reach the next stage of its evolution and finally became ready for use. We started to write technical documentation and notes there. But found, that we need a way to get it from Space somehow.

Currently, Space does not provide a way to just download a directory (I think the feature is worth adding), so I decided to create an application to do that using Space API.

Implementation

The code is available in the repo. You can start with it.

The Space SDK is still pretty unstable and has some bugs, but it is quite easy to use and much more intuitive, than, say, Google Cloud. I will try to walk you through it.

Stage 1. Creating application

First, you need to create a Space application to provide access to API. Go to Administration -> Applications and create a new one. You need to set up permissions like it is shown here. It is not always obvious which permissions you need and error messages are sometimes misleading. For example, I spent some time wondering why I get a zero response for a correct request only to find out, that I need to manually add permissions to restricted projects. But I think they will clean it in the future.

The authentication is always the hardest thing, so I went with the default way (using just the client Id and a secret), but it proved to be surprisingly easy.
.

You can try the APIs in the playground in the same section and get some insight into how they work.

Stage 2. Set up the project with Space SDK.

Just take my build file as a reference.

repositories { mavenCentral() maven("https://maven.pkg.jetbrains.space/public/p/space/maven")
} val ktorVersion = "1.6.4" dependencies { implementation("io.ktor:ktor-client-core:$ktorVersion") implementation("io.ktor:ktor-client-cio:$ktorVersion") implementation("io.ktor:ktor-client-auth:$ktorVersion") implementation("org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-cli:0.3.4") implementation("org.jetbrains:space-sdk-jvm:98244-beta") implementation("ch.qos.logback:logback-classic:1.2.10") testImplementation(kotlin("test"))
}

Be sure to use the correct Ktor version. For me 2.0.0 did not work since SDK has a dependency on it.

You don't need anything else.

Stage 3. Setting up the connection.

It is surprisingly easy:

 val space: SpaceClient = SpaceClient( ktorClientForSpace(CIO), SpaceAppInstance( clientId ?: System.getProperty("space.clientId"), clientSecret ?: System.getProperty("space.clientSecret"), spaceUrl ), SpaceAuth.ClientCredentials() )

Stage 4. Doing logic.

The whole logic is in this file. You can see there are no new classes (look, Ma, I am a functional programmer!).

The SDK is intuitive and follows the API logic. So you just take a client and see methods and properties that are available. Like this:

projects.documents.folders.subfolders.listSubfolders(projectId, folderId)

Sometimes it does not work as expected, so you have to experiment. For example I had to do this:

 val documents = projects.documents.folders.documents.listDocumentsInFolder(projectId, folderId) { id() } documents.data.forEach { val document = projects.documents.getDocument(projectId, it.id) { id() title() documentBody() bodyType() } downloadDocument(directory, document) }

because listDocumentsInFolder returns documents without bodies (it is rational, but not obvious from API).

Stage 5. A hacky part.

The document download went rather smoothly. But no we have markdown documents with attachment links that are:

  • relative,

  • point to the internet files that require authentication.

In order to fix that, we need to download those images and replace links by local file references.

Currently, Space does not provide API to download files (or at least I did not find it), so I had to do a little bit of reverse engineering.

I checked the requests that are used to access images and found out that they have an additional authorization header Bearer. The same one, the Space client uses to access pages:

So all I need to download images is to manually construct the request:

 val response = ktorClient.request<HttpResponse> { url("${server.serverUrl}/d/$imageId") method = HttpMethod.Get header(HttpHeaders.Authorization, "Bearer ${token().accessToken}") }

It is a hack and the actual placement of files could change in the future, but it works for now.

All that is left is to find image references and replace them with text in downloaded markdown files.

That's all.

Conclussion

I hope you will enjoy playing with Space API and I hope there will be a lot of new plugins and applications to enrich the ecosystem. I enjoyed it. Especially in comparison with older heavyweight systems. Kotlin SDK simplifies things a lot.

A bonus

Since we needed not only to download the documents but also to create a report from them, here is the simple PowerShell script to combine documents into a report, using PanDoc:

Items.Title: 创建一个通过滚动出现和消失的标题 | Create a header that appears and disappears by scrolling
Items.Description:
标题可以通过 CSS 和一些 JavaScript 轻松动画，但速度将保持不变。 我讨厌这样，所以我将创建一个像这样自然工作的标题。

示例代码和演示

此脚本通过滚动更改top的值。

 const header = document . querySelector ( " header " ), headerStyle = window . getComputedStyle ( header ), headerHeight = parseFloat ( headerStyle . height ); let lastPosition = 0 ; document . addEventListener ( " scroll " , () => { const currentPosition = window . scrollY , diff = currentPosition - lastPosition ; let newTop = parseFloat ( headerStyle . top ) - diff ; if ( diff < 0 ) { newTop = Math . min ( newTop , 0 ); } else { newTop = Math . max ( newTop , 0 - headerHeight ); } header . style . top = ` ${ newTop } px` ; lastPosition = currentPosition ; });

如果您的标题有阴影，您还需要计算它。

 newTop = Math . max ( newTop , 0 - headerHeight - shadowHeight )

弗朗西斯科·门德斯—— Apr 24 4月24日



原文:

Headers can be animated easily by CSS and a few JavaScript, but the speed will be constant. I hate that, so I’ll create a header that works naturally like this.

Sample code & Demo

This script changes the value of top by scrolling.

const header = document.querySelector("header"), headerStyle = window.getComputedStyle(header), headerHeight = parseFloat(headerStyle.height); let lastPosition = 0; document.addEventListener("scroll", () => { const currentPosition = window.scrollY, diff = currentPosition - lastPosition; let newTop = parseFloat(headerStyle.top) - diff; if (diff < 0) { newTop = Math.min(newTop, 0); } else { newTop = Math.max(newTop, 0 - headerHeight); } header.style.top = `${newTop}px`; lastPosition = currentPosition;
});

If your header has a shadow, you also need to calculate that.

newTop = Math.max(newTop, 0 - headerHeight - shadowHeight)

Francisco Mendes - Apr 24
Items.Author: akimon658

Items.Title: 保护您的 SaaS 应用程序 | Securing Your SaaS Applications
Items.Description:
发表于云计算服务为组织提供了多种能力，包括提供 SaaS 解决方案的能力。 但是，使用端到端的安全解决方案保护托管在公共云上的 SaaS 应用程序是一项艰巨的任务。 这可以归因于您对基础架构或部署在应用程序上的安全解决方案类型的控制有限。 覆盖应用程序漏洞并保护它们免受数据泄露和其他攻击变得至关重要。 您可以遵循以下几点来提高 SaaS 应用程序的安全性。
在 SDLC 中包含安全性

为了赶上最后期限，大多数工程师往往会忽略应用程序安全的重要性，而将注意力集中在其功能上。 这种疏忽可能会使您的应用程序容易受到攻击。 因此，将应用程序安全性与开发一起包含在 SDLC 中。 如果您遵循敏捷或 DevOps 方法，请确保安全补丁是每个版本指南的一部分。

  1. 重视合规性证书 确保您的 SaaS 服务提供商拥有安全应用程序开发所需的认证。 这些证书要求他们进行仔细的审核，以确保您的数据和应用程序始终安全并受到保护。 这些证书还要求他们将网络架构、应用程序开发、策略和数据迁移纳入其安全范围。
  2. 执行严格的漏洞测试 请求您的云服务提供商库中的最新、高端漏洞和事件响应工具。 我们建议使用可以评估系统和应用程序弱点的全自动测试解决方案。 再次强调，如果您基于 Agile 和 DevOps 工作，我们建议对每个版本执行漏洞测试。
  3. 实施用户级数据和应用程序安全 这包括在用户、客户或员工试图访问您的应用程序时部署的安全措施。 您可以使用身份和访问管理工具，该工具允许您保留密码策略、多因素身份验证和其他类似规定。 这将确保只有那些被授权使用您的应用程序的人才能访问它。
  4. 选择虚拟私有云 即使您选择使用 Amazon AWS 等公共云服务提供商构建和托管您的 SaaS 应用程序，您仍然可以订阅虚拟私有云，与公共云相比，您可以更好地控制您的数据。 如果您选择这样做，加密的 Internet 协议安全 (IPsec) VPN 连接将流量路由到您的虚拟私有云中的实例以及从这些实例路由流量。

选择 SaaS 模型的好处，再加上强大的应用程序安全计划，可以为您的运营效率创造奇迹。 在 CloudNow Technologies，我们运用我们在云安全方面的专业知识来提供云咨询服务或云咨询服务，以帮助您确定 SaaS 应用程序所需的正确安全解决方案。 要了解更多信息，请立即与我们联系。



原文:

suresh430

Posted on Apr 27

Cloud computing services provide organizations with several capabilities, including the ability to provide SaaS solutions. However, securing your SaaS applications hosted on a public cloud with an end-to-end, security solution is an uphill task. This can be attributed to the fact that you have limited control over the infrastructure or the type of security solution deployed over your application. It becomes crucial to cover application vulnerabilities and protect them from data breaches and other attacks. Here are a few points you could follow to improve SaaS application security.
Include security in the SDLC

In a rush to meet deadlines, most engineers tend to overlook the importance of application security and focus instead on its capabilities. This oversight might leave your application vulnerable to attacks. Therefore, include application security in the SDLC along with development. If you are following the Agile or DevOps methodology, ensure that security patches are part of every release’s guidelines.

  1. Give importance to compliance certificates Ensure that your SaaS service providers have the necessary certifications for safe application development. These certificates require them to perform careful audits to ensure your data and applications are safe and protected at all times. These certificates also require them to bring network architecture, application development, policies and data migration into their security scope.
  2. Perform rigorous vulnerability tests Request for the latest, high-end vulnerability and incident response tools in your cloud service provider’s arsenal. We suggest going for fully automated testing solutions which can assess system and application weaknesses. Once again, if you are working based on Agile and DevOps, we suggest performing a vulnerability test with every release.
  3. Enforce user-level data and application security This includes security measures that would be deployed when a user, client or employee tries to gain access to your application. You could use an identity and access management tool which allows you to keep in place a password policy, multi-factor authentication, and other similar provisions. This will ensure that only those who are authorized to use your application can have access to it.
  4. Opt for virtual private clouds Even if you choose to build and host your SaaS application with public cloud service providers like Amazon AWS, you can still subscribe to a virtual private cloud which gives you more control over your data than a public cloud. Should you choose to go for this, an encrypted internet protocol security (IPsec) VPN connection routes traffic to and from the instances in your virtual private cloud.

The benefits of choosing a SaaS model, when complemented by a robust application security plan, can do wonders for your operational efficiency. At CloudNow Technologies, we apply our expertise in cloud security to provide cloud consulting services or Cloud advisory services to help you identify the right security solutions that your SaaS applications need. To know more, get in touch with us now.
